items.summary: Stripe built a transformer-based foundation model trained on tens of billions of transactions — treating payments as a language with semantic meaning. Not a text LLM adapted for fraud; it was trained natively on transaction token sequences.
items.source: Jon Eisenstein (FIS) M2020 conversation + Stripe Sessions 2025 + industry analysis

details.what: Stripe's Payments Foundation Model — a self-supervised transformer architecture trained on transaction data, not text. Treats payment sequences as tokens with semantic meaning, plots transactions in hundred-dimensional vector space.
details.architecture: Transformer-based foundation model. Self-supervised learning on transactional data. Processes transactions as token sequences (like words in sentences). Clusters transactions by similarity (same issuer, shared email, etc.) to find structural relationships.

performance.card_testing_detection: 59% → 97% overnight (vs 2-year slog to 80% with prior specialized models)
performance.fraud_detection_improvement: 64% improvement in card-testing attack detection on large businesses overnight

details.key_insight_from_jon: Jon Eisenstein (FIS) said Stripe 'trained an LLM not on text, but on transactions' and the performance is 'stellar — accuracy rates jump from mid-30s to mid-80s.' FIS Project Wolverine was doing the same thing internally and outperformed Falcon by double digits before being disbanded in restructuring.

revaly_implications.threat_level: MEDIUM-HIGH — Not a direct competitor to RTN, but validates the approach of training AI natively on payment data rather than adapting general models
strategic_relevance: Stripe's model proves transaction-native AI dramatically outperforms adapted text LLMs for fraud — this is the same thesis behind RTN's data enrichment approach
Stripe processes transactions across industries/countries and has recurring card number interactions — massive data moat
The foundation model is being deployed across Stripe's entire stack, not just fraud — auth optimization, currency conversion, smart retries — this is where it overlaps with Revaly
Stripe is enabling agentic AI transactions (per Jon's agentic commerce timeline) which creates new fraud patterns the model hasn't trained on yet — potential weakness
Revaly's unique data asset (merchant CRM + fraud tool + acquirer data aggregated) is NOT something Stripe has — they see their side only
revaly_implications.opportunity: Stripe validates the 'language of payments' thesis. Revaly's cross-ecosystem data (issuer + merchant + fraud tool + CRM) could theoretically train an even more powerful model because it sees BOTH sides of the transaction. This is an investor narrative weapon.
revaly_implications.risk: If Stripe moves deeper into issuer-side data (which they're starting to via their issuing product), they could encroach on RTN's value prop from the merchant side up.

public_announcements: Stripe Sessions 2025 (May 2025) — Full public disclosure of foundation model
Gautam Kedia (Head of Applied ML) detailed the architecture
Emily Sands (Head of Information) confirmed performance keeps improving
Chris Skinner / Linas Beliūnas / The Stack all published deep analyses May 2025
details.fis_parallel: FIS Project Wolverine built same approach — 'trained on the language of fraud, not English adapted for fraud.' Achieved 38% → 80%+ accuracy. Team was largely disbanded in July 2025 restructuring. Jon doesn't know current status. Orphaned IP inside FIS.

items.summary: Mica is building a new global payment network from scratch — credential-less, flat per-transaction pricing (not basis points), agnostic to payment type/channel/value. $20M raised (ICE investor). Just partnered with UATP (Feb 2026). Direct competitor to Visa/MC rails long-term.
items.source: Jon Eisenstein (FIS) M2020 referral + mica.io + press releases

details.what: Mica — a new global payment network built from scratch. Not a layer on top of existing rails. Their own protocol designed to compete with card networks.

founder.name: Christopher Petersen
founder.background: 20+ year payments veteran. Built 4 payment processors. Authored 15 patents. Designed first network tokenization solution (cited by Visa/MC as inspiration for theirs). Seven successful exits to Google, Microsoft, FIS, multiple IPOs. Google Wallet/GPay product & business vision. FIS operations worldwide. Bakkt solutions architecture. Carnegie Mellon MBA.
founder.credibility: Jon Eisenstein (who built Google's predecessor products) vouched for him: 'Christopher is quite talented... sold one of his companies to Google, became GPay.'

company.team_size: ~20-30 people (as of Oct 2025 per Jon; likely growing post-$20M raise)
company.ceo: Mike Ward (Christopher is Founder, separate CEO)

funding.latest_round: $10M (March 2025) led by Moonshots Capital + MaQro Advisors

technology.core_innovation: UUEK (Universally Unique Ephemeral Key) — patented credential-less tokenization. Single-use keys that are statistically impossible to duplicate. No stored credentials = no credential fraud, no account takeover.
capabilities: Credential-less payments (no 16-digit card numbers in the flow)
Multi-tender orchestration (split payment across cards, loyalty, stablecoins, A2A in one transaction)
SKU-level adjudication (age verification, filtered spend, EBT within transaction)
Digital coupon redemption embedded in transaction flow
Interactive digital receipts in issuer app
Flat per-transaction pricing (NOT basis points — doesn't care about transaction size)
technology.agnostic_to: Use case (payments, P2P, bill pay, closed loop, EBT, remittances), Channel (online, wallet, in-app, in-store), Value type (fiat, crypto, loyalty, coupons, closed loop)

uatp.what: UATP (global closed-loop network for airlines/travel) partnered with Mica for multi-tender credential-less payments for merchants. Supports stablecoins, A2A, traditional methods. Covers in-store, in-airport, in-flight.
uatp.significance: First major network partnership. Validates the technology in production. Travel/retail vertical entry.

revaly_implications.direct_threat: Not a direct competitor to Revaly today. Mica is rebuilding payment RAILS, Revaly is optimizing DECISIONS on existing rails.
strategic_relevance: If Mica's rails gain traction, the credential-less model eliminates much of the fraud that creates Revaly's recovery opportunity (no stolen cards = fewer false declines to fix)
Mica's flat per-transaction pricing model (vs basis points) could disrupt the economic model that funds fraud tools and recovery services
Jon Eisenstein's timeline: agentic commerce mainstream by end of 2027, stablecoins/new rails gaining domestic traction — Mica is positioned for this
However: card rails have massive inertia. Mica is tiny ($20-30M raised vs Visa's $500B+ market cap). Multi-decade displacement timeline.
Revaly's near-term thesis (3-5 year exit window) is safe. This is a 10+ year structural risk to monitor.
revaly_implications.opportunity: Mica could actually become a DATA PARTNER — their credential-less system still needs fraud/risk intelligence. If they grow, they'll need Revaly-type data enrichment on their rails too. New rails still need trust signals.
revaly_implications.key_question: Does Mica's credential-less model actually eliminate the false decline problem? Or does it create new forms of friction that Revaly could solve?

competitive_position_vs_card_networks.vs_visa_mc: Direct challenge — flat fees per transaction, zero credential fraud by design, multi-tender native
competitive_position_vs_card_networks.vs_fis_fiserv: Complementary/competitive — FIS building tokenized deposits on private L1 blockchain (50K-1M TPS), Fiserv has FIUSD stablecoin. Different architectures toward similar end state.
competitive_position_vs_card_networks.vs_stripe: Overlap on enabling new payment types (stablecoins, A2A) but Stripe works ON existing rails while Mica replaces them

items.summary: Chargebacks911 CEO Monica Eaton warns of new chargeback category emerging from agentic commerce: "I didn't buy that, my AI did." Visa confirms completed secure AI-initiated transactions with partners for 2026 rollout. Potential disruption to traditional dispute categories.

AdvantageCS partnership with Revaly highlighted in industry publication coverage. Partnership combines subscription billing platform capabilities with Revaly's payment recovery expertise to enhance digital payment recovery outcomes.

Chargebacks911 reports global chargebacks volume will increase 41% from 238M to 337M between 2023-2026. 72% of merchants reported increased friendly fraud in 2024. Market opportunity continues expanding.

2Checkout blog reports smart retry timing (dunning management) can recover many failed payments and cut involuntary churn. Most renewal failures aren't true cancellations but temporary issues. Validates payment recovery value proposition.

Kount using ML/AI for fraud detection, learns from new fraud techniques. Multiple sources cite 99% fraud decisions under 400ms, potential 72% chargeback reduction and 46% false decline reduction. Strong competitive benchmark.

Tuhk highlighted as a Canadian fintech startup to watch in 2026, building a collaborative payments platform that enables secure, real-time data sharing between merchants, banks, and service providers to combat global fraud. Direct competitor positioning around data rails.

Paddle is bringing cancellation flows directly into the Paddle Billing dashboard in early 2026, signaling continued investment in their Retain product. Claims 25-30% reduction in customer churn through smart card retries and automated retention offers.

Chargebacks911 reports that 32.4% of merchants use 3-D Secure, 17.8% use RDR (Rapid Dispute Resolution), and 26.3% use pre-chargeback alerts to prevent chargebacks. Low adoption rates suggest opportunity for better solutions.
Chargeflow raised $35M Series A funding and won G2's 2026 Best Software Awards for best commerce software products. Strong momentum for AI-powered chargeback automation competitor.

Payment service providers now standardizing agentic AI for automatic route selection and retries to maximize approval rates. Systems automatically selecting routes and retries to maximize approval rates while controlling cost.
Kount is currently transitioning to Equifax, which may bring additional data resources for fraud prevention capabilities. Noted in Signifyd alternative comparison.

CatalystPay recommends intelligent retry logic that staggers attempts over days rather than hammering the same card repeatedly for VAMP compliance. Proactive communication when payments fail with grace periods before access restrictions.
Chargebacks911 warns merchants to prepare for surge in post-holiday disputes as Lunar New Year spending accelerates across Asia-Pacific and international eCommerce markets.
New promotional video for Revaly created by Burnwe, describing it as 'a payment performance management platform.' Professional marketing push with video content.

AdvantageCS, a major subscription billing platform, announced partnership with Revaly to enhance digital payment recovery. Integration provides intelligent retry guidance for failed transactions to reduce involuntary churn.
Global chargebacks volume projected to increase by 41% between 2023 and 2026, from 238 million to 337 million disputes. Creates significant market opportunity for payment recovery solutions.

Visa excessive chargeback threshold will drop from 2.2% to 1.5% for merchants in North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific starting March 2026. Tighter regulatory environment increases pressure on payment recovery.

Kount (acquired by Equifax 2021) claims 99% fraud decisions delivered in under 400ms, potential to reduce chargeback rates by 72% and false declines by 46%. Strong competitive benchmark for AI-powered fraud prevention.
Industry reports AI-powered smart retries can automatically recover up to 41% of failed payments without coding. Setting market expectations for recovery performance.
Chargebacks911 reports serving over 18,000 companies and notes 72% of merchants experienced increased friendly fraud chargebacks in 2024. Demonstrates scale of chargeback management market.
